headline: Form 5472 for UK Residents Who Own a U.S. LLC: What You Actually Need to File
description: If you're a UK resident who owns a Wyoming, Delaware, or any other US single-member LLC, the IRS requires you to file Form 5472 every year — even if you never made a penny. Here's exactly what to file, when, and what happens if you miss it.
keywords: form 5472 UK residents, UK LLC owner IRS filing, foreign owned US LLC UK, form 5472 disregarded entity UK

mainEntity.name: Do I have to file Form 5472 if my Wyoming LLC made no money?

acceptedAnswer.text: Almost certainly yes, if you funded the LLC at all. The initial capital you put in — even a small amount to open a bank account — is a reportable transaction under 26 CFR §1.6038A-4. The only scenario where you genuinely have no filing obligation is a year in which there were zero transactions between you and the LLC in either direction.

mainEntity.name: My LLC has a UK bank account as well as a US account. Does that change anything?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. It doesn't matter where the LLC's accounts are held. What matters is whether there were transactions between you (as the foreign owner) and the LLC. Transfers between a UK account held by the LLC and your personal UK account are still reportable transactions.

mainEntity.name: I missed the Form 5472 deadline. Can I still file?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. File as soon as possible under the DIIRSP procedure (Delinquent International Information Return Submission Procedure). You attach a reasonable cause statement to the late return explaining why you didn't file on time. The IRS reviews these individually. Filing late with a strong statement is far better than not filing — continuation penalties under IRC §6038A(d)(2) accumulate at $25,000 per 30-day period after an IRS notice.

mainEntity.name: Do I need a US EIN to file Form 5472?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Your LLC must have an Employer Identification Number (EIN) before you can file Form 5472 and the pro forma Form 1120. Foreign owners without an EIN typically apply by fax or by calling the IRS international line at +1-267-941-1099.

mainEntity.name: Do UK LLC owners have to file a BOI report with FinCEN?

acceptedAnswer.text: No — not for a US-formed LLC. As of March 26, 2025, FinCEN exempted all US-formed entities (Wyoming LLCs, Delaware LLCs, etc.) from Beneficial Ownership Information reporting. The BOI requirement now applies only to foreign-formed companies registered to do business in a US state. A UK resident who owns a Wyoming or Delaware LLC is not affected.

itemListElement.name: Form 5472 for UK Residents With a U.S. LLC
